zh.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer於1600年10月26日在英國可瓦爾奎瑟頓可受洗。他於大約1620年與Catherine Chapin在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1677年5月在美國維吉尼亞州素利享年76歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州諾福爾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer于1600年10月26日在英国可瓦爾奎瑟頓可受洗。他于大约1620年与Catherine Chapin在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1677年5月在美国弗吉尼亚州素利享年76岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州諾福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer was christened on 26 October 1600, in Quethiock, Cornwall, England. He married Catherine Chapin about 1620, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in May 1677, in Surry, Virginia, United States, at the age of 76, and was buried in Norfolk, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer a été baptisé le 26 octobre 1600, à Quethiock, Cornouailles, Angleterre. Il a épousé Catherine Chapin environ 1620, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en mai 1677, à Surry, Virginie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à Norfolk, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer wurde am 26. Oktober 1600 in Quethiock, Cornwall, England getauft. Er hat um 1620 in England Catherine Chapin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb im Mai 1677 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Surry, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Norfolk, Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer è stato battezzato il 26 ottobre 1600 nel Quethiock, Cornwall, England. Lui sposato Catherine Chapin circa 1620, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel maggio 1677, nel Surry, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolto a Norfolk, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencerは1600年10月26日にイギリスコルンワルクエスイオックで洗礼を受けた。約1620年にイギリスでCatherine Chapinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1677年5月にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州スリーで76歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ノーフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.Norfolk, Virginia, British Colonial Amer: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 노르폴크
ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer(은)는 1600년 10월 26일 영국 코른월 크스이오c크에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1620년 영국에서 Catherine Chapin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1677년 5월 미국 버지니아 주 수리에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 노르폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer foi batizado em 26 de outubro de 1600, em Quethiock, Cornwall, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Catherine Chapin aproximadamente 1620, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em maio de 1677, em Surry, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, com 76 anos, e foi sepultado em Norfolk, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer был крещен 26 октября 1600 г. в местности куетиокк, корнвалл, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Chapin около 1620 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер май 1677 г. в местности сурри, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности норфолк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer se bautizó el 26 de octubre de 1600, en Quethiock, Cornwall, Inglaterra. Se casó con Catherine Chapin aproximadamente 1620, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en mayo de 1677, en Surry, Virginia, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultado en Norfolk, Virginia, British Colonial America.
